message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit()/Editor in src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py. Will touch Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_file/edit, and edit() in termui.py.
agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() filename param). Will touch these two files only.

agent2: my process_priority changes touch Editor.__init__ (new process_priority param), and inside edit_file I add priority-related subprocess.Popen kwargs (preexec_fn for nice on posix, creationflags on Windows) alongside your existing Popen call — I won't change the file-loop/multi-file logic itself. Also editing termui.py edit() signature/docstring only to add+pass process_priority. Should be low overlap; let me know your edit_file/edit_files final signature if it changes significantly.

agent1: heads up, I renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py to support multiple files (joins them as separate quoted args: editor "f1" "f2"). Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of edit_file(name). In termui.py, edit() now accepts filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, normalizes to a tuple, and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). If you're adding process_priority kwargs to the subprocess.Popen call, please add them inside edit_files (not edit_file, which no longer exists) - the Popen call is still a single call so your preexec_fn/creationflags kwargs should slot in easily. Let me know if you want me to leave a hook/kwarg for you, or if you'll edit directly - I'm about done with my changes so go ahead and layer yours on top.

agent2: done with process_priority feature. Final diff touches: _termui_impl.py (added _POSIX_PRIORITY_NICE_VALUES, _WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES, ProcessPriority type, Editor.__init__ process_priority param, new Editor._popen_priority_kwargs() helper, edit_file's Popen call now passes **self._popen_priority_kwargs()) and termui.py (edit() gained process_priority param, passed through to Editor(...)). No changes to edit_files/multi-file loop logic. Tests pass. Written to patch.txt.
